title: Write a LiveView on_mount sandbox hook
prompt: Write a module `MyAppWeb.UserAuth` at `lib/my_app_web/user_auth.ex` that defines `on_mount(:sandbox, params, session, socket)` which: (1) calls `Phoenix.LiveView.get_connect_info(socket, :user_agent)` to extract the user-agent header, (2) decodes sandbox metadata via `Phoenix.Ecto.SQL.Sandbox.decode_metadata(user_agent)`, (3) calls `Phoenix.Ecto.SQL.Sandbox.allow(metadata, Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox)`, and (4) returns `{:cont, socket}`. Handle the nil-user-agent case safely.
tier_rationale: Multi-step integration — needs knowing get_connect_info, decode_metadata, and allow/2.
research_citation: "The sandbox uses the mechanic described here to track the caller (aka the test process) for a LV" — Elixir Forum, LostKobrakai
